slides.title: Revenue quality pressure points
slides.strapline: Concentrated churn, price resets, and working-capital drag are tightening quality of revenue in FY2025.

body.text: Top ten customers represent 61% of recurring revenue, increasing renewal concentration risk.

children.text: Two enterprise contracts are in active rebid cycles and include downside volume clauses.
Commercial teams estimate a 3-5 point margin drag if these contracts reset below current floor pricing.
children: Sensitivity case assumes only partial volume recovery by H2 FY2026 and slower expansion conversion.
Gross dollar retention dropped to 86% as repriced legacy cohorts renewed at lower committed volume.

body.text: Upfront discounting accelerated ACV conversion in Q4 but pulled margin pressure into subsequent quarters.
children: Implementation overruns on three strategic accounts triggered service credits and delayed milestone billing.
Working-capital conversion weakened as invoice disputes increased on custom scope statements.

body: Base-case underwriting should ring-fence near-term revenue confidence to contracted backlog and apply explicit downside sensitivity to FY2026 growth assumptions.
slides.source: Source: CRM cohort extract, contract register, and monthly bridge pack (FY2024-FY2025).

slides.title: Risk and mitigation priorities
slides.strapline: Execution risk is manageable if commercial controls and delivery governance are tightened in the first 90 days.

leftBody.text: Repricing cadence is inconsistent across enterprise cohorts.
children: Non-standard discount terms are approved late and reduce renewal visibility.

children.text: Pipeline staging quality is weak in two regions with repeated close-date slippage.
children: Owner-level escalation is inconsistent and forecast confidence declines near quarter-end.

leftBody.text: Utilization volatility in implementation teams has increased rework.
children: Scope drift now extends cash conversion on fixed-fee projects by roughly three weeks.

rightBody.text: Introduce approval guardrails for non-standard terms with weekly exception review.
children: Require deal-margin checkpoints before final quote issuance.
Standardize renewal playbooks by cohort and renewal type.

rightBody.text: Deploy account-level burn monitoring and milestone gating.
children: Escalate accounts when labor mix or timeline variance exceeds threshold before billing confidence is affected.

slides.title: Growth slowed after pricing reset
slides.strapline: Revenue growth decelerated after the FY2024 price reset while churn remained elevated in lower-margin cohorts.

body.text: Quarterly revenue growth declined from 18% to 9% across the period.
children: The steepest deceleration appears in cohorts exposed to contract rebasing.

children.text: Churn stayed above long-run average after Q2 FY2025 despite improved new-logo bookings.
children: Momentum from new logos was offset by weaker expansion and lower realized price on renewals.

body.text: Discount-heavy renewal tactics preserved volume but compressed realized price.
children: Expansion contribution from existing customers decreased as pricing exceptions widened.

body: Investment case should separate temporary pricing actions from structural demand and model a staged recovery path tied to renewal quality improvements.

chart.source: Source: Management monthly KPI pack, cohort bridge, and revenue analytics cube.
